title: Atlas Brand System
subtitle: A flexible identity for a spatial design studio.
description: A brand system built around modular grids, precise typography, and a restrained palette for a studio working across architecture and interiors.

blocks.text: The identity needed to feel measured without becoming cold. We started with a typographic grid that could hold project imagery, client proposals, and site documentation in one consistent visual language.

blocks.alt: Atlas brand guideline spread with modular layout blocks
blocks.caption: Guideline spread showing the spacing logic and image rhythm.

left.text: The previous identity worked for small presentations but collapsed when the studio needed proposals, signage, and social assets at different speeds.

right.text: We created a flexible grid, a compact type scale, and image rules that let the team assemble new material without rebuilding layouts each time.

steps.title: Audit
steps.description: Mapped existing proposal decks, invoices, moodboards, and presentation exports to find repeatable structure.

steps.title: Identity Logic
steps.description: Defined mark usage, layout behavior, and an image treatment that could flex across print and digital formats.

steps.title: Guideline Kit
steps.description: Delivered editable templates and a compact usage guide for internal project teams.

items.label: Template formats
items.description: Proposal, case study, stationery, and social layouts.

items.label: Production time
items.description: Estimated reduction for recurring presentation work.

items.label: Core grid
items.description: One layout logic across most project documents.

blocks.title: Outcome
items: A sharper studio presence across client-facing documents.
A practical template system for internal team use.
A visual foundation that can support future web and signage work.
